events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 95,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing check valve on boiler return or supply piping allowing gravity flow.",
    "Uncontrolled gravity circulation can lead to boiler short-cycling, overheating, and thermal shock.",
    "Non-conformance with standard hydronic piping practices requiring check valves to prevent thermo-siphoning."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate and drain piping section.",
    "Install spring-loaded check valve in correct orientation per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Refill, purge air, and test system for proper flow direction and boiler operation."
  ],
  "rationale": "A missing check valve in a hydronic system permits gravity circulation, causing heat migration, energy waste, and potential boiler damage. This is a known defect and a code/manual violation. The system is not acceptable for continued operation without correction.",
  "references": [
    "Boiler OEM installation manual (check valve requirement)",
    "ASHRAE Handbook – HVAC Systems and Equipment (hydronic piping design)",
    "International Mechanical Code (IMC) Section 1003.3.2 backflow/check provisions"
  ]
}
